help.card.eagle:
If you do not have the color of the landscape led, then you must play an Eagle card, if you have any.
You can choose to play an Eagle card in 2 different ways:

<0>FLEE</0>: Either the eagle flees, and in that case, your opponent wins the trick. The Eagle card goes to the scoring pile of the player who played it.

<0>FIGHT</0>: Or the eagle fights, and in that case, you trump your opponent's card. You win the trick. The Eagle card is played as a trump.

If you play an Eagle card to lead a trick, you must fight, you cannot flee. Your opponent must follow suit with an Eagle card, if they have any. They cannot flee, and the Eagle card with the higher value wins the trick.
help.card.mouse:
<0>Power</0>: The Mouse card beats the Elephant card of the same color (same landscape) if both cards are played during the trick.
 
The player who has played the Mouse card wins the trick.
help.card.cheetah: <0>Power</0>: Decide who will lead the next trick. If two Cheetah cards are played during the same trick, the second one is the one to decide.
help.card.elephant: The Elephant has a weakness against the Mouse.
help.card.snake:
<0>Power</0>: Choose one of your opponent's available cards and block it (place it horizontally: the card is blocked) for their next turn. Once your opponent has played another card, the card is no longer blocked. 

If the player decides to block a card from their opponent's hand, they choose one without looking at it.

The last available card of a player cannot be blocked.
help.card.rhino: <0>Power</0>: Move a card placed on top of one of your opponent's piles and place it under any other of their piles (while leaving it face-up or face-down), whatever the number of cards in the pile.
help.card.hyena: <0>Power</0>: If you take all four Hyena cards, you immediately lose the round!

help.help.title: Help card
help.help.a:
<0>SCENARIO A</0>
If you have the corresponding suit, you must <1>follow</1> suit.
help.help.b:
<0>SCENARIO B</0>
If you don't have the corresponding suit but have an Eagle, you must play an Eagle.
help.help.c:
<0>SCENARIO C</0>
If you have neither the corresponding suit nor an Eagle card, you must <1>discard</1> a card.
tuto.intro: <0>The lion is dead...</0> Who, between the black panther and the white tiger, will take his place?
tuto.objective:
You are playing the <0>White Tiger</0>. Your objective is to win 2 rounds before your opponent.
This card will be flipped to its golden side when you win a first round.
tuto.intro.1:
Awimbawé is <0>a trick-taking game</0>: for each trick, the first player plays one of their available cards. Then the opponent does the same.
The player who wins the trick collects the 2 cards and becomes the first player for the next trick.
tuto.intro.2: Once all the cards have been played, the round ends. The player with <0>the most crowns</0> on the cards in their tricks <0>wins the round</0>!
tuto.hand: Here are <0>the cards in your hand</0>. The opponent does not know them. <0>They are available</0> to be played.
tuto.piles:
Here are <0>the cards in your stacks</0>. The 4 top cards are <0>visible to both players and available</0>: you can play them.
The 4 bottom cards will only be revealed and available after you've played the card on top.
tuto.elephant:
The player with the fewest crowns visible on the 4 cards placed in front of them starts the game.
It's your turn: play this <0>Elephant</0> by placing it in the middle of the table.
tuto.color: Each card has a <0>color</0>. Your Elephant has a green background (grassland), your opponent must play a card of the same color if possible.
tuto.rhino: The opponent has played a <0>Rhinoceros</0> from their hand.
tuto.power: When a card is played, <0>its power applies immediately</0>. The Elephant doesn't have a power, but the Rhinoceros does: it allows you to move a card from an opponent's stack and place it under another stack!
tuto.rhino.effect: Your opponent has moved this card. They're trying to limit your playing options!
tuto.solve.1:
The Elephant has a value of 6, and the Rhinoceros a value of 2, so you've won the trick.
Now it's your turn to play again.
tuto.block.1: The snake allows you to <0>block one of your opponent's cards</0>: it cannot be played in their next move.
tuto.block.2:
It's possible to block a card from a stack, or a random card in their hand.
Block this <0>Eagle</0>.
tuto.eagle: Your opponent must play a green card. If they don't have any, <0>they must play an Eagle if possible</0>.
tuto.eagle.opponent:
Your opponent has played an Eagle from their hand.
The Eagle has a double power: the player who plays it can either <0>Fight</0> or <0>Flee</0>.
tuto.eagle.choice:
If the Eagle <0>Fights</0>, it wins the trick.
If the Eagle <0>Flees</0>, the opponent wins the trick, but <0>you keep the Eagle in your tricks</0>.
tuto.eagle.fight: The opponent has chosen to <0>Fight</0>, so they have won the trick. Now it's their turn to play.
tuto.yellow-elephant: Your opponent has played the yellow Elephant.
tuto.solve.2:
The opponent wins the trick because the Elephant is stronger than the Cheetah, however the Cheetah's power <0>allows you to choose who starts the next turn</0>.
Choose to start.
tuto.eagle.start: When you play an Eagle to lead the trick, your opponent <0>has to play an Eagle</0> if possible, and <0>the 2 Eagles have to fight</0>, they cannot flee.
tuto.green-mouse:
Now play your green Mouse.
The Mouse scores 3 crowns, and <0>automatically defeats the Elephant of its suit</0>.
tuto.discard:
If your opponent doesn't have the suit led (green), or an Eagle, <0>they must discard</0> by playing any other card.
<0>They then automatically lose the trick</0> even if the card played is stronger.
tuto.hyena.loose:
Hyenas score more crowns, but <0>beware: if you pick up all 4, you immediately lose the round!</0>
For this reason, the Hyena cards remain visible in the tricks.
tuto.reminder:
At any time, you can click on a card for a reminder of the rules.
Now it's your turn to play, and good luck!
